skill_name: First-session safety assurance for acute psychotic fear of dying

supports.quote:
Following Freud's advice on technique,I do not use process notes. I do make some notes in the first session and once in a while if I think it will be helpful. Freud felt that taking notes

Two University Deans contacted me about Mr.X during the early months of his treatment. One wanted to know whether there was anything the Dean might do to help. I suggested he call the patient, ask him that question,and take seriously anything the patient said.

At some time in the first session,as I often do with patients who are feeling terrified, I said I would not killhim or let anyone else kill him. This is based on an observation by Bruno Bettelheim (in a 1954 colloquium at Princeton University) that every first psychotic episode is accompanied by an acute conscious fear of dying. In fact, in my experience, most schizophrenics are consciously aware of such a fear. This patient neither acted as if that were very helpful nor as if it were strange. Probably he did not believe me, but he did not say so.

metadata.skill_name: First-session safety assurance for acute psychotic fear of dying
metadata.objective: Reduce acute terror associated with first psychotic episode by directly naming and containing death anxiety
sibling_headings: # 3. GUIDING CONCEPTION WITH RESEARCH AND CLINICAL EXPERIENCE SUPPORT

metadata.section_summary: In this era most mental health professionals do not know that any schizophrenic ever got better with psychotherapy. Of course, there are a group of professonals, members of “ISPS"
